taskDescription: Drag the correct terms into the blanks to complete each paragraph about histology and embryology of the abdomen and pelvis.
textField:
The GIT wall has a universal four-layer structure: mucosa, submucosa, *muscularis externa*, and serosa or adventitia. *Brunner's* glands in the submucosa are the distinguishing histological feature of the duodenum, while *Peyer's* patches (aggregated lymphoid follicles) distinguish the ileum.

The epithelium of the GIT is derived from *endoderm*, while the surrounding connective tissue and muscle are from splanchnic *mesoderm*. The liver and pancreas develop as endodermal outgrowths (buds) of the *foregut*.

*Parietal* cells in the fundic glands of the stomach secrete hydrochloric acid and *intrinsic factor*, which is essential for vitamin B12 absorption. Chief cells in the same glands secrete *pepsinogen*.

The kidney develops from *intermediate* mesoderm in three successive waves: pronephros, *mesonephros*, and metanephros. *Hirschsprung's* disease results from failure of migration of neural crest cells to the distal bowel, causing *aganglionosis*.

In the absence of testosterone, the *Mullerian:also called paramesonephric* duct system persists to form the female reproductive tract, including the uterine tubes, uterus, and upper vagina.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review histology and embryology of the abdomen.
Good effort! Review the details you missed.
